name: Wild Rice-Pine Nut Stuffing
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Pine nuts or any chopped nut
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the wild rice in the water until tender, about 1 hour. Add more water if needed.
2. In a separate pan, sauté the onion and garlic in vegetable oil until fragrant.
3. Add the pine nuts or chopped nut to the pan and cook for an additional minute.
4. Stir in the dried thyme and wild rice that has been cooked.
5. Simmer together for 10 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
6. Let the stuffing cool sufficiently to handle before using it to stuff quail, one Cornish hen, or serving alongside chicken.
1/2 recipe - 182 calories, 1 bread exchange, 2 fat (15 grams), 4 grams carbohydrate, 4 grams protein, 11 grams fat (138 mg sodium, 114 mg potassium, 0 cholesterol
Source: Am. Diabetes Assoc. Holiday Cookbook by Betty Wedman, 1986 Shared but not tested by Elizabeth Rodier, Nov 93
Note: Pine nuts are quite expensive, approximately five times the cost of shelled sunflower seeds in the bulk bin. They have a mild flavor profile.
